codeReview.summary: Feature flag infrastructure implemented with full management, instant rollback, and gradual rollout support. 29 unit tests pass covering all core functionality. Build passes successfully.

issues.description: RolloutStrategy::Phased is defined with RolloutPhase containing min_iterations field, but the strategy is never used in is_enabled_for() - the method only checks rollout_percentage. The comment on line 85 says 'for future extensibility'. This is not a blocking issue since percentage-based gradual rollout works correctly, but the phased strategy infrastructure is currently unused dead code.

test_phased_rollout_strategy test uses rollout_percentage=100 which causes is_enabled_for() to return true immediately (line 214-216: if self.rollout_percentage >= 100 { return true; }). The test passes but does not actually verify phased rollout functionality works - it only verifies the flag can be created with a Phased strategy.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Implementation follows existing patterns - thiserror for errors, tracing for logging, standard HashMap usage. No new conventions introduced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: FeatureFlagError uses thiserror::Error, methods use info!/warn! tracing macros, flags stored in HashMap<String, FeatureFlag> matching other manager patterns in codebase.

summary: Feature flag infrastructure for instant rollback is implemented and functional. Core requirements met: flag management (create/get/list/enable/disable/delete), instant rollback via snapshots, gradual rollout via percentage. The Phased rollout strategy is defined but not wired into is_enabled_for() - this is non-blocking since percentage-based rollout works correctly. All 29 tests pass and build succeeds.
